public_reputation: Known around Soreheim Proper through day-to-day work as innkeeper.
appearance: Liora Hearthwrit carries the marks of innkeeper in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Reads people fast, keeps hospitality practical, and measures danger by what it does to ordinary routine. In Soreheim Proper, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of innkeeper in and around Soreheim Proper, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Soreheim Proper while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Liora Hearthwrit can draw the party into a locality problem centered on innkeeper duties in Soreheim Proper.
Liora Hearthwrit becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with quota demands, political rivalry, foreign pressure around Soreheim Proper.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Liora Hearthwrit is immediately readable as a innkeeper tied to Soreheim Proper.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Soreheim Proper: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Liora Hearthwrit presents as someone shaped by the duties of a innkeeper and the daily pressures of Soreheim Proper.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a innkeeper, and proof that strangers will not make Soreheim Proper harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Liora Hearthwrit refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a innkeeper too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can surface gossip, shelter, and the social temperature of a place without formal inquiry.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Liora Hearthwrit adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Liora Hearthwrit always knows more about Soreheim Proper than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a innkeeper in Soreheim Proper.
social_texture: Liora Hearthwrit speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Soreheim Proper, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Liora touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Liora Hearthwrit when the party needs a socially grounded face for Soreheim Proper: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
